title: CBDT Releases Key Updates from Draft Income-tax Rules, 2026: A Quick Look
content:
# CBDT Releases Key Updates from Draft Income-tax Rules, 2026: A Quick Look

Ek important update hai! Central Board of Direct Taxes (CBDT) ne Income-tax Act, 2025 ke liye draft Income-tax Rules, 2026 aur usse related forms publicly available kar diye hain. Ye rules 1st April, 2026 se effective honge aur inka aim hai tax compliance ko aur bhi simplified banana.

**Key Updates from the Draft Rules:**

*   **Simplified Framework:** The new rules propose a significant reduction in the number of rules from 511 to 333 and forms from 399 to 190, making the tax regime less complex.
*   **"Smart Forms" Introduction:** CBDT has designed "smart forms" with features like automated reconciliation and pre-fill capabilities to make filing more intuitive and reduce errors.
*   **Perquisite Valuations Revised:**
    *   Free food and non-alcoholic beverages ki exemption limit INR 50 per meal se badhakar INR 200 per meal kar di gayi hai.
    *   Gifts, vouchers, or tokens ki perquisite value ab nil hogi if the aggregate value does not exceed INR 15,000 during the tax year (earlier limit was INR 5,000).
*   **TDS Forms Consolidation:** Form No. 26QB, 26QC, 26QD, and 26QE ko consolidate karke ab ek single Form No. 141 mein file karna hoga for various TDS transactions, simplifying the process for co-owners, co-buyers, etc.
*   **Form 16 Replacement:** Old Form No. 16 ko ab Form No. 130 replace karega as the certificate for tax deducted at source on salary, pension, or interest paid to specified senior citizens.
*   **CA Verification for FTC:** Agar aap INR 100,000 ya usse zyada ka Foreign Tax Credit (FTC) claim kar rahe hain, toh ab Form No. 44 ko Chartered Accountant se verify karana compulsory hoga.
*   **Safe Harbour Regime (SHR) Overhaul:**
    *   SHRs ab 3-year block period ke liye apply honge, starting from Tax Year 2026-27.
    *   IT services ke liye uniform operating profit margin 15.5% set kiya gaya hai (earlier it ranged from 17% to 24%).
    *   Transaction value threshold for SHRs has been substantially increased from INR 300 crore to INR 2,000 crore.

These draft rules were put in the public domain for stakeholder feedback, and the consultation period ended on February 22, 2026. The final rules are expected to be notified soon.

## Read the Official Document
*   [Download the Official Notification/Circular here](https://eportal.incometax.gov.in/iec/foservices/#/pre-login/ita-comprehensive-review)

*Published on: 6 March 2026*
